parameters.text:
Hello! I’m your AI Learning Assistant.  
I can help you: 
- learn from materials shared via URLs 
- get concise summaries 
- take quizzes based on studied materials  

Commands: 
- /learn [url] 
- /quiz

Please send a URL in this format: 
/learn https://example.com/article
=The URL appears to be invalid. Please provide a full valid link.
I couldn’t extract enough useful content from this page. Please try another URL.

options.systemMessage:
You are Teacher, an AI learning assistant that analyzes study material provided by the user.

Your task is to read the provided material and produce a structured learning summary.

Rules:
- Use only the information from the provided material.
- Do not invent facts that are not supported by the material.
- Make the summary specific to this material, not generic.
- Identify the most important concepts and ideas.
- Determine the difficulty level as exactly one of:
  beginner, intermediate, advanced
- Return between 5 and 7 key points.
- Keep the wording clear and easy to understand.
- Return only valid JSON.
- Do not include markdown fences.
- Do not include any text before or after the JSON.

Return JSON in this exact structure:
{
  "title": "string",
  "difficulty": "beginner | intermediate | advanced",
  "keyPoints": ["string", "string", "string"],
  "mainConcepts": ["string", "string", "string"]
}

parameters.text: Would you like to test your understanding?

options.systemMessage:
You are Examiner, an AI assessment assistant.

Your task is to create a multiple-choice quiz based only on the provided learning material.

Rules:
- Use only the provided material.
- Do not invent facts that are not supported by the material.
- Create exactly 5 questions.
- Each question must test understanding of the specific material, not general background knowledge.
- Each question must have exactly 4 answer options labeled A, B, C, and D.
- Only one option must be correct.
- Provide the correctAnswer as one of: A, B, C, D.
- Provide a short explanation for why the correct answer is correct.
- Return only valid JSON.
- Do not include markdown fences.
- Do not include any text before or after the JSON.

Return JSON in this exact structure:
{
  "questions": [
    {
      "id": "Q1",
      "question": "string",
      "options": {
        "A": "string",
        "B": "string",
        "C": "string",
        "D": "string"
      },
      "correctAnswer": "A | B | C | D",
      "explanation": "string"
    }
  ]
}

parameters.text:
You don’t have any saved learning materials yet.

Use /learn [url] first to add a topic.
This quiz session is no longer active. Please start a new quiz with /quiz.

parameters.query:
SELECT
  qa.session_id,
  qa.selected_answer,
  qa.is_correct,
  qq.id AS question_id,
  qq.question_order,
  qq.question_text,
  qq.options_json,
  qq.correct_answer,
  qq.explanation,
  qs.quiz_id
FROM quiz_answers qa
JOIN quiz_questions qq
  ON qa.question_id = qq.id
JOIN quiz_sessions qs
  ON qa.session_id = qs.id
WHERE qa.session_id = $1
ORDER BY qq.question_order ASC;

SELECT
  q.id AS quiz_id,
  lm.title
FROM quizzes q
JOIN learning_materials lm
  ON q.material_id = lm.id
WHERE q.id = $1
LIMIT 1;

additionalFields.text: This question is no longer active.
